signals.headline: Bank of Korea Holds Rates at 2
signals.raw:
Bank of Korea Holds Rates at 2.5%** | Already Priced In | HIGH confidence[6]
The Bank of Korea made this decision on April 10, 2026, maintaining its policy rate unchanged amid geopolitical uncertainty and rising inflation pressures.

signals.headline: South Korea's March 2026 Inflation at 2
signals.raw:
South Korea's March 2026 Inflation at 2.2% Year-over-Year** | Confirmed | HIGH confidence[2][4]
Annual inflation rose from 2.0% in February to 2.2% in March, driven primarily by rising global oil prices and transportation costs increasing 5% year-over-year.

signals.headline: ADB Raises 2026 Inflation Forecast to 2
signals.raw:
ADB Raises 2026 Inflation Forecast to 2.3%** | Confirmed April 10 | HIGH confidence[5]
The Asian Development Bank revised its inflation projection upward by 0.2 percentage points due to Middle East tensions and currency weakness, while raising growth to 1.9%.
